title: CB Cover Scenario
description: You are a centre-back. A wide opponent has the ball. Your fellow CB is pressing. Find the correct covering position to protect the central channel.
teaching_point: Step inside and deeper to protect the central channel while your fellow CB presses wide.

feedback_hints.success: Well positioned — you covered the central channel and protected against the through ball.
feedback_hints.common_error: You followed your partner too wide, leaving the central channel exposed.
feedback_hints.alternate_valid: This was still a defensible position even outside the ideal zone.
feedback_hints.teaching_emphasis: When your partner presses wide, your job is to protect the central channel — not to shadow the ball.
success_points: You dropped inside to protect the central channel while your partner pressed wide.
Your covering angle gave the goalkeeper a second line of defence against the through ball.
You stayed goalside and central, denying the striker a clean run in behind.
error_points: You drifted too wide, shadowing your partner instead of protecting the central channel.
Your position was too high, leaving space in behind for a through ball.
You left the central corridor unguarded by moving away from the cover position.
alternate_points: You were slightly outside the ideal pocket but still provided useful central cover.
The line of defence was still intact from this position, even if not optimal.
